title: Molten Winey Russian Deodeok Root Pierogi
description: A hearty, comforting dish from Russia featuring a rich wine and onion filling, infused with the earthy sweetness of deodeok root, and topped with a sweet and sticky glaze. Perfect for a cozy evening in.
instructions: Combine deodeok root, flour, warm water, salt, sugar, and melted butter in a large mixing bowl. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a golf ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of the onion and red wine filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, and carefully add the pierogi to the pot.
Cook the pierogi for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and red wine mixture to the butter, and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the filling is heated through and the onions are caramelized.
Serve the pierogi hot, topped with the sweet and sticky glaze.

To make the glaze, combine the sugar and red wine in a small saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the glaze has thickened slightly.
Drizzle the glaze over the pierogi before serving.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh dill if desired.
